_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for which host owns which secret. Read by BOTH the encryption layer (scripts/lib/secrets.sh, via python json) and the Nix sops modules (via builtins.fromJSON). Editing this file is the only way to change host->secret ownership: the per-host encryption scope AND the sops.secrets read-selectors both derive from `hosts.<name>.secrets`, so they cannot drift. `catalog` maps each secret key to how its plaintext is produced at encryption time: kind=scalar -> `leaf: <json-quoted env var>`; kind=perhost -> `leaf: <json-quoted env var named <var>_<HOST-UPPER>>` (a distinct value per host, e.g. the per-node Tailscale auth key minted into TS_AUTHKEY_HERMES / TS_AUTHKEY_METAL); kind=envblock -> `leaf: |` then indented VAR=value lines. bluebubbles owns no secrets (no age key, no bundle) and is the reference-isolated node.
